channel.title: Tags on Rectangular Drawer
channel.description: Recent content in Tags on Rectangular Drawer

item.title: Vim
item.description:
Vim’s Note

Vim’s Configurateion location :

~/.config/nvim/init.vim (Unix and OSX)

To make init.vim loaded with root user.

$ cp $HOME/username/.config/nvim/init.vim /root/.config/nvim/

PREREQUISITES :

nodejs, git, nvim, vim-plug

“echo $HOME” to check the home directoray

Mode

  • Normal - ESC
  • Insert - “k” to insert before the block
  • Visual - v on Normal mode

Move

  • “n” to go left
  • “i” to go right
  • “e” to go down
  • “u” to go up

Word and Block Move

  • “w” to move to the next word
  • “b” to move to the last word
  • “0” to move to the first place on the line
  • “G” to move to the last line, “gg” to the first line
  • “K” go to the start of the line
  • “A” go to the end of the line

Write

  • “p” to paste
  • “y” to copy (same command as “d” on “)
  • “k” to insert
  • “S” to save
  • “Q” to quit, [if you want to quit without save, then type “:q!” (“ZZ” for “:q!”)]
  • “R” to reload the $MYVIMRC file

Delete

There is no delete in vim, all is cut and put things in clipboard

  • “x” to delete the word on the cursor
  • “c” to delete the word on the cursor and insert/edit on the current place.
  • “d” to cut
  • “yk”” copy everything in ""
  • “ckw” / change in word, in general
  • “ck’x’” / change in ‘x’, for example `"’ for’x’, then it will delete everything in ""
  • “dk”" delete everything in ""

  • “d” plus command,
  • “d” plus/+ navigation, delete one word on the way it gave.
  • “d” plus/+ “number” plus/+ navigation = delete “num” word on the direction
  • double “d” to delete/cut the current line,

Search

  • Search on vim : “/”
  • disable higlightSearch / :nohlsearch (space + enter)
  • “f + ‘x’” / “fv” to find the first word start with “v” on current line
  • “df:” to delete till “:”, “yf:” to copy till “:” , and so on

Split Screen

  • “:split” for vertical split, “:q”/Q to quit (su, se)
  • “:vsplit” for horizontal split, “q”/Q to quit (sn, si)
  • “set splitright” then “vsplit”, to move the cursor to the right side(<LEADER + i/n>)
  • “sh” to transform vsplit to split. (up & down)
  • “sv” to transform split to vsplit. (left & right)

Visual Mode

  • “shift + v” to select as line
  • “ctrl + v” to enter the “Visual Block” mode
  • after select, press":" the type [mode][command] (:normal AfinsHere)
  • it would add “finsHere” in the end of every line selected
    (after selected all words on block, press “shift+k” to insert, this change would apply to all block that have chosen)

<C-w>l //Ctrl+w+l

New Page

  • “:tabe” (tu)
  • “:-tabnext” move left page (tn)
  • “:+tabnext” move right page (ti)

Plugin

  • call plugin#begin()
  • Plugin ‘{adress of the plugin/github(https)}’ (git needed)
  • dall plugin#end()

COC.NVIM

Nodejs, vim-plug

:help coc-nvim

Misc.

  • :e ~/Desktop/a.py (e for edit, to edit a file on vim)
  • :set spell! (to enable spelling mistake check)
  • ,z=, to open possible correction
  • :x, to save and exit

set clipboard=unnamedplus (to share the clipboard with system)
Nvim_terminal_emulator, :split term://bash or :tabnew term: (bash)

To be expanded.

item.title: Setting Up
item.description:
Something about how i build up this site

Prerequisites

  • git
  • hugo

I would probably say only these two are neccessary

Content

So first go into a directory where you want to put your site files at then run the command

hugo new site "sitename"

and, clone whatever theme you like to the directory called theme (You could find out on hugo’s website or somewhere else on the internet.)

Edit the file called config.toml, i just simply copy the Sample Configuration. from the theme’s wiki.

then, run

hugo

under your siet’s root directory to genarate your site, the files would be at ./public directory (don’t forget to run this command everytime after you make changes or add new contents.)

Also I have put this site on github page, you can see it by the domain. so what i did is create a repository with “xxx.github.io” as it’s name, it seems only by doing that can you use github page feature. (you may also need to enable this feature on repo’s setting.)

then run

git init

under the public directory since thoes files in it are all you need.

run

hugo server

to check the website changes in realtime, i do edit on WSL, the default server address it given just not works for me, so what i run is

hugo server --bind WSLIP --baseURL=http://WSLIP --noHTTPCache

(You can get the WSLIP by using the command below)

ip a

Next you just need to do git push, which i won’t talk about here, so that’s all.
